core_mechanism: the cavity method — a self-consistent fixed-point recursion for marginal distributions on a locally tree-like sparse random structure (equivalent to Bethe–Peierls / belief-propagation message passing), extended with a one-step replica-symmetry-breaking ansatz that counts and weights the pure states (clusters) of a solution space and thereby locates a cascade of sharp critical thresholds where that space shatters into exponentially many clusters, condenses onto a few dominant ones, and freezes a finite fraction of variables
brief_reason: The abstract obtains all results 'using the cavity method' to analyze the space of solutions of a random constraint-satisfaction problem, exposing successive clustering, condensation and freezing transitions (with computed critical connectivities) analogous to the mean-field theory of glasses as the constraint density increases.
